D1 Optics L5 Evaluate Reference

Faraday Rotation & Optical Isolators

rotate polarization with a magnetic field and exploit its non-reciprocity

A longitudinal magnetic field makes a medium rotate polarization. Crucially the sense is fixed by the field, not the propagation direction, so a round trip doubles rather than cancels the rotation.

Set the magnetic field B and path length L. Toggle the return pass to see the rotation double — the non-reciprocity that makes a one-way optical isolator possible.
